name: Aditi Garcia
resume_text:
Aditi Garcia
Email: aditi.garcia@example.com | Current Role: Senior React Developer
Location: Seattle, WA

PROFESSIONAL PROFILE:
Detail-oriented and high-velocity Software Professional with 0.8 years of hands-on experience in modern technology ecosystems. Possesses a proven track record of diagnosing deep technical failures, scaling system limits, and driving engineering excellence across teams. Believer in documentation, robust architecture, and mentoring junior engineers to elevate engineering standards.

KEY ENGINEERING WORK & METRICS:
Led frontend architecture migrations, focusing heavily on modern rendering strategies. Authored reusable design systems in TypeScript using strict generics for maximum type safety. Designed and built our primary application using secure, zero-trust cloud network architecture on AWS using private subnets, VPC peering, and IAM service roles, which succeeded in implementing robust telemetry (Prometheus/Grafana) that reduced Mean Time to Resolution (MTTR) by 50%. Deeply knowledgeable in DOM reconciliation, avoiding stale closures in custom React hooks, and eliminating render-blocking resources. 

Technical Problem Solved:
We hit a wall with CPU bottlenecks during real-time ML inference. I resolved this by compiling the model to TensorRT, batching requests dynamically using Triton Inference Server, and leveraging CUDA stream concurrency.

TECHNICAL SKILLS:
Languages & Frameworks: HTML5, Vite, WebAssembly, TailwindCSS, TypeScript, Webpack, Redux, React, Svelte, GraphQL
Architectures & Practices: Test-Driven Development (TDD), Microservices, CI/CD pipelines, System Telemetry, Cloud Security, Agile Scrum, API Design.

Emma Perez
Email: emma.perez@example.com | Current Role: Lead Backend Engineer
Location: San Francisco, CA

PROFESSIONAL PROFILE:
Detail-oriented and high-velocity Software Professional with 3.8 years of hands-on experience in modern technology ecosystems. Possesses a proven track record of diagnosing deep technical failures, scaling system limits, and driving engineering excellence across teams. Believer in documentation, robust architecture, and mentoring junior engineers to elevate engineering standards.

KEY ENGINEERING WORK & METRICS:
Architected and maintained highly concurrent distributed backend services processing billions of events. Designed our backend services using event-driven architecture using Apache Kafka to orchestrate asynchronous microservices, successfully reducing API latency by 45% using Redis caching and query optimization. Expert in database schema migrations, distributed locking using Redis, and profiling application CPU/memory bottlenecks. Championed clean, test-driven development practices (Jest, PyTest, Go Test) maintaining over 92% code coverage. 

Technical Problem Solved:
A critical memory leak and hydration mismatch caused performance degradation in production. I analyzed the memory profiles using Chrome DevTools, traced the leak to an uncleaned window event listener in a custom React hook, and refactored it safely.

TECHNICAL SKILLS:
Languages & Frameworks: Docker, MongoDB, gRPC, SQL, Spring Boot, Node.js
Architectures & Practices: Test-Driven Development (TDD), Microservices, CI/CD pipelines, System Telemetry, Cloud Security, Agile Scrum, API Design.

LEADERSHIP & ENGINEERING IMPACT:
- Established engineering standards, coding style guidelines, and RFC processes, boosting developer onboarding speed by 40%.
- Mentored and guided 6 junior and mid-level developers, with 3 achieving promotions under my guidance.
- Led cross-functional initiatives to review and deprecate unused cloud assets, saving $120,000 annually in hosting costs.

Deeply familiar with PyTorch internals, custom CUDA kernel optimization, and setting up automated drift monitoring pipelines (MLflow). Architectures & Practices: Test-Driven Development (TDD), Microservices, CI/CD pipelines, System Telemetry, Cloud Security, Agile Scrum, API Design.

Deeply knowledgeable in DOM reconciliation, avoiding stale closures in custom React hooks, and eliminating render-blocking resources. 

Technical Problem Solved:
We hit a wall with CPU bottlenecks during real-time ML inference. I resolved this by compiling the model to TensorRT, batching requests dynamically using Triton Inference Server, and leveraging CUDA stream concurrency.

Languages & Frameworks: React, Cypress, Next.js, Redux, CSS3, HTML5, Vue.js, Jest, JavaScript, TypeScript
Architectures & Practices: Test-Driven Development (TDD), Microservices, CI/CD pipelines, System Telemetry, Cloud Security, Agile Scrum, API Design.
